children.heading: Making Slots Available for Essential Air Service.—
children.label: a Making Slots Available for Essential Air Service.—

children.heading: .—Operational authority
children.label: 1 .—Operational authority
children.text: If basic essential air service under subchapter II of this chapter is to be provided from an eligible point to a high density airport (other than Ronald Reagan Washington National Airport), the Secretary of Transportation shall ensure that the air carrier providing or selected to provide such service has sufficient operational authority at the high density airport to provide such service. The operational authority shall allow flights at reasonable times taking into account the needs of passengers with connecting flights.

children.heading: .—Exemptions
children.label: 2 .—Exemptions
children.text: If necessary to carry out the objectives of paragraph (1), the Secretary shall by order grant exemptions from the requirements of subparts K and S of part 93 of title 14, Code of Federal Regulations (pertaining to slots at high density airports), to air carriers using Stage 3 aircraft or to commuter air carriers, unless such an exemption would significantly increase operational delays.

children.heading: .—Assurance of access
children.label: 3 .—Assurance of access
children.text: If the Secretary finds that an exemption under paragraph (2) would significantly increase operational delays, the Secretary shall take such action as may be necessary to ensure that an air carrier providing or selected to provide basic essential air service is able to obtain access to a high density airport.

children.heading: .—Action by the secretary
children.label: 4 .—Action by the secretary
children.text: The Secretary shall issue a final order under this subsection on or before the 60th day after receiving a request from an air carrier for operational authority under this subsection.

children.heading: Slots for Foreign Air Transportation.—
children.label: b Slots for Foreign Air Transportation.—

children.heading: .—Exemptions
children.label: 1 .—Exemptions
children.text: If the Secretary finds it to be in the public interest at a high density airport (other than Ronald Reagan Washington National Airport), the Secretary may grant by order exemptions from the requirements of subparts K and S of part 93 of title 14, Code of Federal Regulations (pertaining to slots at high density airports), to enable air carriers and foreign air carriers to provide foreign air transportation using Stage 3 aircraft.

children.heading: .—Slot withdrawals
children.label: 2 .—Slot withdrawals
children.text: The Secretary may not withdraw a slot at Chicago O’Hare International Airport from an air carrier in order to allocate that slot to a carrier to provide foreign air transportation.

children.heading: .—Equivalent rights of access
children.label: 3 .—Equivalent rights of access
children.text: The Secretary shall not take a slot at a high density airport from an air carrier and award such slot to a foreign air carrier if the Secretary determines that air carriers are not provided equivalent rights of access to airports in the country of which such foreign air carrier is a citizen.

children.heading: .—Conversions of slots
children.label: 4 .—Conversions of slots
children.text: Effective , slots at Chicago O’Hare International Airport allocated to an air carrier as of , to provide foreign air transportation shall be made available to such carrier to provide interstate or intrastate air transportation.May 1, 2000November 1, 1999

children.heading: .—Slots for New Entrants
children.label: c .—Slots for New Entrants
children.text: If the Secretary finds it to be in the public interest, the Secretary may by order grant exemptions from the requirements under subparts K and S of part 93 of title 14, Code of Federal Regulations (pertaining to slots at high density airports), to enable new entrant air carriers to provide air transportation at high density airports (other than Ronald Reagan Washington National Airport).

children.heading: Special Rules for Ronald Reagan Washington National Airport.—
children.label: d Special Rules for Ronald Reagan Washington National Airport.—
A
children.text: result in an increase in the total number of slots per day at Ronald Reagan Washington National Airport;

children.label: B
children.text: result in an increase in the total number of slots at Ronald Reagan Washington National Airport from 7:00 ante meridiem to 9:59 post meridiem;

children.label: C
children.text: increase the number of operations at Ronald Reagan Washington National Airport in any 1-hour period by more than 2 operations;

children.label: D
children.text: result in the withdrawal or reduction of slots operated by an air carrier;

children.label: E
children.text: result in a net increase in noise impact on surrounding communities resulting from changes in timing of operations permitted under this subsection; and

children.label: F
children.text: continue in effect on or after the date on which the final rules issued under subsection (f) become effective.

children.heading: .—Limitation on applicability
children.label: 2 .—Limitation on applicability
children.text: Nothing in this subsection shall adversely affect Exemption No. 5133, as from time-to-time amended and extended.

children.label: A
children.text: whether improvements in technology and procedures of the air traffic control system and the use of quieter aircraft make it possible to eliminate the limitations on hourly operations imposed by the high density rule contained in part 93 of title 14 of the Code of Federal Regulations or to increase the number of operations permitted under such rule;

children.label: i
children.text: congestion and delay in any part of the national aviation system;

children.label: ii
children.text: the impact of noise on persons living near the airport;

children.label: iii
children.text: competition in the air transportation system;

children.label: iv
children.text: the profitability of operations of airlines serving the airport; and

children.label: v
children.text: aviation safety;

children.label: C
children.text: the impact of the current slot allocation process upon the ability of air carriers to provide essential air service under subchapter II of this chapter;

children.label: D
children.text: the impact of such allocation process upon the ability of new entrant air carriers to obtain slots in time periods that enable them to provide service;

children.label: E
children.text: the impact of such allocation process on the ability of foreign air carriers to obtain slots;

children.label: F
children.text: the fairness of such process to air carriers and the extent to which air carriers are provided equivalent rights of access to the air transportation market in the countries of which foreign air carriers holding slots are citizens;

children.label: G
children.text: the impact, on the ability of air carriers to provide domestic and international air service, of the withdrawal of slots from air carriers in order to provide slots for foreign air carriers; and

children.label: H
children.text: the impact of the prohibition on slot withdrawals in subsections (b)(2) and (b)(3) of this section on the aviation relationship between the United States Government and foreign governments, including whether the prohibition in such subsections will require the withdrawal of slots from general and military aviation in order to meet the needs of air carriers and foreign air carriers providing foreign air transportation (and the impact of such withdrawal on general aviation and military aviation) and whether slots will become available to meet the needs of air carriers and foreign air carriers to provide foreign air transportation as a result of the planned relocation of Air Force Reserve units and the Air National Guard at O’Hare International Airport.

children.heading: .—Report
children.label: 2 .—Report
children.text: Not later than , the Secretary shall complete the current examination of slot regulations and shall transmit to the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate and the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure of the House of Representatives a report containing the results of such examination.January 31, 1995

children.heading: .—Rulemaking
children.label: f .—Rulemaking
children.text: The Secretary shall conduct a rulemaking proceeding based on the results of the study described in subsection (e). In the course of such proceeding, the Secretary shall issue a notice of proposed rulemaking not later than , and shall issue a final rule not later than 90 days after public comments are due on the notice of proposed rulemaking.August 1, 1995

children.heading: .—Weekend Operations
children.label: g .—Weekend Operations
children.text: The Secretary shall consider the advisability of revising section 93.227 of title 14, Code of Federal Regulations, so as to eliminate weekend schedules from the determination of whether the 80 percent standard of subsection (a)(1) of that section has been met.

children.heading: .—Commuter air carrier
children.label: 1 .—Commuter air carrier
children.text: The term “commuter air carrier” means a commuter operator as defined or applied in subpart K or S of part 93 of title 14, Code of Federal Regulations.

children.heading: .—High density airport
children.label: 2 .—High density airport
children.text: The term “high density airport” means an airport at which the Administrator limits the number of instrument flight rule takeoffs and landings of aircraft.

children.heading: .—New entrant air carrier
children.label: 3 .—New entrant air carrier
children.text: The term “new entrant air carrier” means an air carrier that does not hold a slot at the airport concerned and has never sold or given up a slot at that airport after , and a limited incumbent carrier.December 16, 1985

children.heading: .—Slot
children.label: 4 .—Slot
children.text: The term “slot” means a reservation for an instrument flight rule takeoff or landing by an air carrier of an aircraft in air transportation.

children.label: A
children.text: “40” shall be substituted for “12” in sections 93.213(a)(5), 93.223(c)(3), and 93.225(h);

children.label: i
children.text: “slot exemptions”;

children.label: ii
children.text: slots operated by an air carrier under a fee-for-service arrangement for another air carrier, if the air carrier operating such slots does not sell flights in its own name, and is under common ownership with an air carrier that seeks to qualify as a limited incumbent and that sells flights in its own name; or

children.label: iii
children.text: slots held under a sale and license-back financing arrangement with another air carrier, where the slots are under the marketing control of the other air carrier; and

children.label: C
children.text: for Ronald Reagan Washington National Airport, the Administrator shall not count, for the purposes of section 93.213(a)(5), slots currently held by an air carrier but leased out on a long-term basis by that carrier for use in foreign air transportation and renounced by the carrier for return to the Department of Transportation or the Federal Aviation Administration.

children.heading: .—Regional jet
children.label: 6 .—Regional jet
children.text: The term “regional jet” means a passenger, turbofan-powered aircraft with a certificated maximum passenger seating capacity of less than 71.

children.heading: .—Nonhub airport
children.label: 7 .—Nonhub airport
children.text: The term “nonhub airport” means an airport that had less than .05 percent of the total annual boardings in the United States as determined under the Federal Aviation Administration’s Primary Airport Enplanement Activity Summary for Calendar Year 1997.

children.heading: .—Small hub airport
children.label: 8 .—Small hub airport
children.text: The term “small hub airport” means an airport that had at least .05 percent, but less than .25 percent, of the total annual boardings in the United States as determined under the summary referred to in paragraph (7).

children.heading: .—Medium hub airport
children.label: 9 .—Medium hub airport
children.text: The term “medium hub airport” means an airport that each year has at least .25 percent, but less than 1.0 percent, of the total annual boardings in the United States as determined under the summary referred to in paragraph (7).

children.label: A
children.text: the names of the airports to be served;

children.label: B
children.text: the times requested; and

children.label: C
children.text: such additional information as the Secretary may require.

children.label: A
children.text: approve the request if the Secretary determines that the requirements of the section under which the request is made are met;

children.label: B
children.text: return the request to the applicant for additional information relating to the request to provide air transportation; or

children.label: C
children.text: deny the request and state the reasons for its denial.

children.heading: 60-.—day period tolled for timely request for more information
children.label: 3 60-.—day period tolled for timely request for more information
children.text: If the Secretary returns under paragraph (2)(B) the request for additional information during the first 20 days after the request is filed, then the 60-day period under paragraph (2) shall be tolled until the date on which the additional information is filed with the Secretary.

children.heading: .—Failure to determine deemed approval
children.label: 4 .—Failure to determine deemed approval
children.text: If the Secretary neither approves the request under paragraph (2)(A) nor denies the request under paragraph (2)(C) within the 60-day period beginning on the date the request is received, excepting any days during which the 60-day period is tolled under paragraph (3), then the request is deemed to have been approved on the 61st day, after the request was filed with the Secretary.

children.heading: .—Exemptions May Not Be Transferred
children.label: j .—Exemptions May Not Be Transferred
children.text: No exemption from the requirements of subparts K and S of part 93 of title 14, Code of Federal Regulations, granted under this section or section 41716, 41717, or 41718 may be bought, sold, leased, or otherwise transferred by the carrier to which it is granted, except through an air carrier merger or acquisition.

children.heading: .—Affiliated Carriers
children.label: k .—Affiliated Carriers
children.text: For purposes of this section and sections 41716, 41717, and 41718, an air carrier that operates under the same designator code, or has or enters into a code-share agreement, with any other air carrier shall not qualify for a new slot or slot exemption as a new entrant or limited incumbent air carrier at an airport if the total number of slots and slot exemptions held by the two carriers at the airport exceed 20 slots and slot exemptions.
